item.title: How Russia Put Its Future at Risk by Remaking Its Economy for War
item.description: About half of the country’s federal budget goes toward the fight in Ukraine, money that does little to support its long-term development.

item.title: China Amps Up Pressure on Japan With Restrictions on Exports
item.description: Beijing placed the restrictions on 20 Japanese entities with ties to the defense industry, the latest ratcheting up of its monthslong feud with Tokyo.

item.title: What to Know in NYC After Blizzard: School Closures, Transportation and More
item.description: Public schools and Broadway shows will be open, but transit and flight disruptions could linger.

item.title: Record Snowfall Slams New England and Leaves New York City Snarled
item.description: At the storm’s peak, well over half a million homes and businesses in the Northeast were without power.

item.title: State of the Union May Be a Trial for ‘Barely Invited’ Justices
item.description: If tradition holds, members of the Supreme Court will attend Trump’s annual speech, just days after ruling against the legality of his tariffs.
